nodes.name: 冰岛
nodes.summary: 冰岛是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联19种鸟类，包括粉脚雁、角鸊鷉、翻石鹬等19种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 西伯利亚
nodes.summary: 西伯利亚是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联73种鸟类，包括红胸黑雁、雪雁、凍原豆雁等73种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 格陵兰
nodes.summary: 格陵兰是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联14种鸟类，包括雪雁、粉脚雁、角鸊鷉等14种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: LC
nodes.summary: LC表示 IUCN 无危等级，当前关联8208种鸟类，如鸵鸟、鸸鹋、南方鶴鴕等8208种鸟类。

nodes.name: 非法猎捕
nodes.summary: 直接捕杀或非法贸易导致种群下降。

nodes.name: 铅中毒
nodes.summary: 食腐鸟类因摄入铅弹残留而中毒。

nodes.name: 农药污染
nodes.summary: 农业化学品通过水体和食物链影响鸟类生存。

nodes.name: 日本
nodes.summary: 日本是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联81种鸟类，包括灰雁、青头潜鸭、花脸鸭等81种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 島嶼
nodes.summary: 島嶼是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联3种鸟类，包括Cape Verde storm petrel、黑脚信天翁、白尾海雕，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 峡谷峭壁
nodes.summary: 大型猛禽和食腐鸟偏好的高崖栖息环境。

nodes.name: 白尾海雕
nodes.summary: 白尾海雕（White-tailed eagle，学名 Haliaeetus albicilla），在图谱中标记为无危（LC）物种，已关联7个分布地点节点，常见栖息地包括湿地、峡谷峭壁，主要威胁包括农药污染、非法猎捕、铅中毒。

nodes.name: 北海道
nodes.summary: 北海道是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联13种鸟类，包括丹顶鹤、赤颈鸊鷉、普通秧鸡等13种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 中亚
nodes.summary: 中亚是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联34种鸟类，包括斑头雁、大鸨、小鸨等34种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 湿地
nodes.summary: 浅水、沼泽与植被斑块组成的高生产力生态系统。

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: Off. While found across a wide range, today breeding from as far west as Greenland and Iceland across to as far east as Hokkaido, Japan, they are often scarce and spottily distributed as a nesting species, mainly due to human activities

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: thumb|right|White-tailed eagles are fairly associated with wooded areas but usually ones quite near water. White-tailed eagles may be found in varied habitat but usually are closely associated with water and generally occurs in lowland areas. Although mainly a lowland species, the species is known to live at elevations of so long as there is water access in some parts of Central Asia and Siberia. In coastal areas, the species may range from high sea cliffs down to low-lying islands and archipelagos

links.label: 栖息于
links.evidence: These have included habitat alterations and destruction of wetlands, about a hundred years of systematic persecution by humans (from the early 1800s to around World War II) followed by inadvertent poisonings and epidemics of nesting failures due to various manmade chemical pesticides and organic compounds, which have threatened eagles since roughly the 1950s and continue to be a potential concern

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: These have included habitat alterations and destruction of wetlands, about a hundred years of systematic persecution by humans (from the early 1800s to around World War II) followed by inadvertent poisonings and epidemics of nesting failures due to various manmade chemical pesticides and organic compounds, which have threatened eagles since roughly the 1950s and continue to be a potential concern

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: Smithsonian Inst Press. Some populations have since recovered well, due to governmental protections, dedicated conservationists and naturalists protecting habitats and nesting sites, partially regulating poaching and pesticide usage, as well as careful reintroductions into parts of their former range.{{cite book |last1=Ferguson-Lees |first1=James |last2=Christie |first2=David A

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: Biological Conservation, 4(5), 373–377. It was estimated that pesticides and metal contaminations reduced the white-tailed eagle population in Hungary from 1957 to 1967 by about 50–60%. Lead poisoning, caused by lead bullets left in carcasses that the white-tailed eagles will eat in winter, is also another issue faced by the species
